name: Sword of Attila
description: The Sword of Attila, also known as the Sword of Mars or the Sword of God, was a legendary weapon said to have been wielded by Attila the Hun. Legend states that it was not forged by human hands but miraculously appeared to Attila. A shepherd found the sword when a heifer grazed and limped on it. The shepherd dug it out of the ground and presented it to Attila. Attila interpreted this discovery as a divine sign that he was destined to conquer the world. The sword became a symbol of his power and authority, bolstering his claim as the scourge of God. It was believed to grant him invincibility and ensure victory in battle. Its blade was said to glow with an unearthly light, striking fear into the hearts of his enemies. The sword represented not just military might but also divine favor, solidifying Attila's position as a ruler chosen by the gods. Its loss would have been a severe blow to Hunnic morale and power. Though its existence is debated among historians, the sword remains a potent symbol of Attila's reign and the fearsome reputation of the Hunnic Empire. It is often depicted as a simple, perhaps crude, but undeniably powerful weapon.
subtitle: The Divine Sword of Conquest
origin: Miraculously discovered, gifted by the gods.

keyMyths.title: Discovery of the Sword
keyMyths.description: A shepherd finds the sword after a heifer injures itself on it, presenting it to Attila as a divine sign.

keyMyths.title: Sword as a Symbol
keyMyths.description: The sword becomes a potent symbol of Attila's divine right to rule and his military prowess.

sources.author: Jordanes
